fundamentals.name: CrowdStrike Holdings, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: CrowdStrike Holdings, Inc. provides cybersecurity solutions in the United States and internationally. Its unified platform provides cloud-delivered protection of endpoints, cloud workloads, identity, and data through a software as a service (SaaS) subscription-based model. The company offers corporate endpoint and cloud workload security, managed security, security and vulnerability management, IT operations management, identity protection, threat intelligence, data protection, SaaS security posture management, and AI powered workflow automation, and securing generative AI workload services, as well as security orchestration, automation, and response; and security information and event management, and log management services. It primarily sells subscriptions to its Falcon platform and cloud modules. The company was incorporated in 2011 and is headquartered in Austin, Texas.

judgment.summary: High-growth cybersecurity company with strong revenue and cash flow, but currently unprofitable and trading at a very high valuation.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimates by 1.6%; next earnings date is 2026-06-03.
positives: Revenue growth 23.3% YoY, with analyst estimates for current Q at 46.2%.
Gross margin 74.8% indicates strong pricing power and efficient operations.
Free cash flow (TTM) of $1.60B shows solid cash generation despite accounting losses.
Total cash of $5.23B provides ample liquidity.
negatives: Forward P/E of 118.46 and PEG ratio of 5.32 indicate a very high valuation.
Profit margin is negative at -3.4%, and return on equity is -4.1%.
Debt/Equity ratio of 18.34 is high, though offset by substantial cash reserves.
Analyst mean target of $550.53 is significantly below the current spot price.

news.paragraph: CrowdStrike is seeing positive news flow ahead of its Q1 earnings report, with one analyst admitting they were wrong and are now buying in, citing AI potential. The company also announced a collaboration with insurers on AI cyber risk, adding to the narrative. While the stock is trading up today, the upcoming earnings report will be key to confirming if the AI thesis is translating into tangible growth.
